__TRANSLATE_0__: Memory Providers for AI Agents — 2026 Guide
__TRANSLATE_7__: Which memory provider should you use?
__TRANSLATE_8__: Eight providers compared — hosting, free tiers, benchmarks, architecture, and a concrete pick for each major use case.
__TRANSLATE_12__: Scroll right on mobile. "Best for" and pricing are the most decision-relevant columns.
__TRANSLATE_55__: Architecture, pricing, tools exposed, and what each provider actually does well.
__TRANSLATE_57__: Hybrid triple-store: vector + key-value + knowledge graph. An LLM pass extracts structured facts from conversations and stores them across all three layers. Most widely integrated option — first-class Python, TypeScript, and OpenAI-compatible SDKs.
__TRANSLATE_59__: Free: 10K adds / 1K recalls · $19/mo Starter · $249/mo Pro (Graph Memory)
__TRANSLATE_62__: all-around use, teams wanting a managed service with a strong SDK and no vendor lock-in (self-host option, Apache license).
__TRANSLATE_63__: TEMPR architecture: four parallel retrieval strategies — temporal, entity, metadata, and BM25 for exact keyword matches. Strong at structured technical recall: port numbers, error codes, service names, deployment configs. Three-stage pipeline: retain (ingest) → recall (retrieve) → reflect (synthesize across stored knowledge).
__TRANSLATE_66__: Full local free · Cloud: $15/M retain · $0.75/M recall · $3/M reflect
__TRANSLATE_71__: coding agents, privacy-first local setups, anyone who wants the best published benchmark scores. Highest LongMemEval score of any provider listed.
__TRANSLATE_72__: CLI: open source (custom license) · cloud: proprietary · 4.2K ⭐
__TRANSLATE_73__: Leads the LoCoMo benchmark — specifically designed for multi-hop and temporal reasoning across long conversation histories. Local CLI is open source and free. Cloud sync for cross-device persistence costs $19/mo. Built with coding agents as the primary use case.
__TRANSLATE_80__: coding agents and autonomous workflows that need multi-hop or temporal reasoning. Strong #2 on LongMemEval-class tasks.
__TRANSLATE_82__: Optimized for search-heavy workloads. Ingests content from many sources and surfaces it via semantic search. Generous free tier (1M tokens). The star count reflects the consumer app frontend — the core memory engine is closed source. Strong LongMemEval score but behind Hindsight.
__TRANSLATE_89__: knowledge bases and chatbots that need to surface relevant content from a large corpus quickly. No self-host option.
__TRANSLATE_90__: MIT · built into Hermes · local SQLite
__TRANSLATE_91__: Uses Holographic Reduced Representations (HRR) algebra on a local SQLite + FTS5 store. Zero external dependencies — no API keys, no network calls, no Docker. Memory lives in a single file in your Hermes home directory. The most private option by definition. The  tool exposes 9 actions: add, search, probe, related, reason, contradict, update, remove, list.
__TRANSLATE_98__: zero-cost local setups, privacy-critical environments, anyone who wants memory working instantly with no accounts or config.
__TRANSLATE_100__: Tiered context loading by resolution depth: L0 loads ~50-token abstracts, L1 loads ~500-token overviews, L2 loads full content on demand. Only the detail level needed for each query gets pushed into the context window — that's the mechanism behind the 80–90% token savings. Self-hosted only, AGPL. Requires Docker and an LLM provider for extraction.
__TRANSLATE_103__: Free to self-host · AGPL means server modifications must be disclosed if distributed
__TRANSLATE_107__: on-prem deployments and regulated industries needing full data sovereignty. 80–90% token reduction is real if you have large memory stores.
__TRANSLATE_109__: Three specialized LLM agents — Deriver (extracts user preferences), Dialectic (surfaces them in context), Dreamer (synthesizes across sessions). The only provider focused on building a persistent user model ("dialect"), not just storing facts. Available as cloud or self-hosted via the AGPL repo.
__TRANSLATE_116__: personal AI companions and multi-user apps where each user's preferences and working style need to shape responses over time.
__TRANSLATE_117__: Proprietary · cloud only · domain status unverified
__TRANSLATE_118__: Database-style memory with structured schema. Explicit control over what gets stored and how it's queried — more like a managed database than an LLM memory layer. No free tier. Domain availability was inconsistent at time of writing — verify before depending on it.
__TRANSLATE_125__: production apps that need structured data recall with predictable query behavior and no LLM extraction overhead.

__TRANSLATE_130__: State across long sessions, multi-hop reasoning, exact technical recall (ports, configs, error codes).

__TRANSLATE_132__: leads LoCoMo (multi-hop 92.2%, temporal 94.4%); local CLI free; purpose-built for coding agents
__TRANSLATE_133__: mature SDK, works well with tool-calling patterns, self-hostable fallback
__TRANSLATE_135__: Indexing a large, growing body of content and surfacing the relevant slice at query time.

__TRANSLATE_137__: built for search-heavy workloads; most generous free tier (1M tokens); good LongMemEval score (81.6%)
__TRANSLATE_138__: solid recall + open Apache license if you want to self-host the index
__TRANSLATE_140__: Best option for most use cases when you don't have a strong constraint pushing you elsewhere.

__TRANSLATE_150__: SQLite file on disk, zero network calls, zero external dependencies — most private by construction
__TRANSLATE_151__: full local mode, MIT, no cloud required

__TRANSLATE_156__: reliable fact recall across sessions; works well for preference tracking even without dedicated user modeling
